response.justification: A strong, experienced generalist engineer, but a poor match for a role whose core requirement is hands-on C++/STL development. The candidate's only C++ exposure is roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a university thesis; C++ does not even appear in the skills list, and the recent decade is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang web and platform work. There is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers, IEC 62443/SDL, industrial automation protocols, or UML architecture documentation. Transferable strengths exist (low-latency async systems, event-driven/distributed platforms, multithreading via DLang fibers, strong TDD/CI-CD craftsmanship), but they do not cover the central technical and security expertise this position demands.
strengths: Deep, broad senior experience with low-latency, event-driven, distributed systems
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development
Demonstrated concurrency/performance work (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput services)
concerns: Minimal and dated C++/STL experience; not listed among core skills
No evidence of MQTT, IEC 62443/SDL, industrial automation, or protocol experience
No UML/architecture-documentation or message-broker background shown
